title: Faith.
content:
Better trust all, and be deceived,
         And weep that trust, and that deceiving;
    Than doubt one heart, that if believed,
         Had blessed one's life with true believing.

    Oh, in this mocking world, too fast
         The doubting fiend o'ertakes our youth!
    Better be cheated to the last,
         Than loose the blessed hope of truth.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Frances Anne Kemble (Fanny), titled "Faith.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
